During a verbal altercation with the manager, Dematteis physically assaulted the manager, causing him to lose control and push him against a bar and an elderly woman on the sidewalk. The incident was witnessed by other customers and has led to Dematteis' arrest and charges of assault.

Gitte Denteneer, a mother in Belgium, received a bill from the Rubens Café that included 50 cents for warming up her baby's feeding bottle. Her tweet about the unusual charge went viral, leading to widespread outrage and accusations of stinginess. Despite the cost, Denteneer's bill was relatively small at around £20, including various food items. The restaurant chain confirmed that such a service fee was indeed charged, but only when explicitly requested by customers.

Manchester City and Chelsea are vying for the services of West Ham's impressive left-back, Aaron Cresswell, particularly in light of City's need to increase their quota of English players. Cresswell, recently signed by West Ham from Ipswich for £2 million, has been successful in Premier League play, attracting interest from both City and Chelsea, who also seek to bolster their homegrown player quotas. Other potential targets for City include Tottenham's Danny Rose and Liverpool's Raheem Sterling, while West Ham is unlikely to sell Cresswell, who is on a long-term deal.

Manchester United has offered goalkeeper David de Gea a massive salary of £200,000 per week to stay at the club, with the prospect of him potentially returning to Spanish club Real Madrid next summer. De Gea's performance has been impressive for Manchester United this season, and while the team has made an offer to keep him, Real Madrid could still lure him away with a free transfer. The 24-year-old has been a standout player for the club and is seen as a potential replacement for Casillas at Real Madrid. Negotiations remain ongoing, and De Gea ultimately holds the final decision.

Ian Bell has extended his contract with Warwickshire until the end of the 2017 season, bringing him to play for another three years. The 32-year-old cricketer has won five trophies with the club, including the County Championship in 2004 and 2012. Despite representing England, Bell is an integral part of the Warwickshire squad, making significant contributions on and off the pitch. He is currently preparing for England's tour of the West Indies and upcoming home series against New Zealand and Australia.

Tyler MacNiven, who rose to fame as a contestant on The Amazing Race, created an entertaining "save the date" video for his upcoming wedding. Inspired by action movies, the 3-minute film features MacNiven and his fiancée, Kelly Hennigan, battling "villains" and surviving a massive explosion. They set their wedding date to September 26, 2015, and include the Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco as part of their ceremony backdrop. The video showcases MacNiven's filmmaking skills, while Kelly's brother, John Morrison, a professional wrestler, contributed to the storyline. The video has received positive reactions, and there's speculation about adding a magnetic version for guests to display at home.

Phil Rudd, the drummer of AC/DC, pleaded guilty to threatening to kill and possession of drugs in a New Zealand court. Previously denied the charges, Rudd made several threatening calls to his personal assistant, which led to his arrest. The court sentenced him to up to seven years in prison for the threat charge, though his trial for attempting to procure murder was dismissed due to lack of evidence. Rudd was initially released on bail pending his upcoming sentencing hearing.

Rebecca Grant, 40, from Biddeford, Maine, faced additional charges after trying to resist arrest. During her transport to jail, she severely damaged a police cruiser by biting it, scratching it, and threatening deputies. Initially suspected of kidnapping, Grant became violent when confronted, resulting in multiple charges including violation of bail conditions and criminal mischief.

A newborn baby girl, born alive and still fully term, was found dead in a garbage can near Muskingum University in Ohio. This incident follows a similar case in 2002 where a baby boy was found in a dumpster. Both incidents happened at the same university, and the Safe Haven Law, allowing parents to leave newborns anonymously, is in place. However, the parents involved were prosecuted for abandoning the children.

Ronaldo, a retired 38-year-old former Brazilian footballer, expressed his desire to play alongside his younger namesake Cristiano Ronaldo if he were still active in the modern era. He believes they would complement each other well, despite acknowledging his own age and fitness would make a comeback unlikely. Ronaldo, who is now an owner of the Fort Lauderdale Strikers, expressed a wishful sentiment about playing alongside Cristiano Ronaldo.

Dan Price, the CEO of Gravity Payments, drastically cut his own salary to 7% of his original $1 million, setting a $70,000 minimum wage across the company. This includes all employees, including customer service representatives and clerks, raising the salaries of nearly half of the 120 staff members. The move, which will cost Price over $900,000 annually, aims to boost morale and align compensation with lower employee incomes, despite being criticized for potentially hurting his personal finances.

Dikembe Mutombo, known for his defensive prowess and signature finger-wagging, was elected to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame. He was a four-time NBA Defensive Player of the Year, leading the league in blocked shots for five consecutive seasons and played in the NBA from 1991 to 2009. Alongside Mutombo, other notable members of the 2015 class included John Calipari, Dick Bavetta, Spencer Haywood, Jo Jo White, and Lisa Leslie, with all 11 members being honored at the Hall of Fame in Springfield, Massachusetts on September 11.

The parents of an infant, Rachel Jaajaa and Aaron Tate, were charged with child endangerment after the baby was found unattended at a McDonald's in Ohio. The couple reportedly argued over who should care for the three-month-old, and Tate left the baby at the restaurant while Jaajaa attempted to leave work. They were both arrested and charged after police responded to a 911 call. Jaajaa's sister now cares for the baby.

Derby County, seeking a new manager after Steve McClaren, are considering Paul Clement, who is currently coaching at Real Madrid and was previously considered for the Newcastle United job. Derby County's interest in Clement stems from McClaren potentially leaving Real Madrid, given McClaren's future at Derby is uncertain. Derby is preparing to make a move on Clement, though it remains to be seen if this will happen. McClaren could remain at Derby if they secure promotion to the Premier League.

Didier Drogba has announced he will continue his playing career next season, despite recent struggles at Chelsea where he has been given limited minutes. The 37-year-old striker made a surprise return to Chelsea last summer and has now revealed his intention to finish his career at the club. Though his future with the team is uncertain due to lack of a new contract, Drogba expressed his desire to stay motivated and win titles for Chelsea.

The seven-year-old British schoolgirl, India Mayhew, died after suffering serious head injuries when the horse she was riding bolted at a holiday destination in India. She was reportedly just ahead of her father and a staff member during the incident, which occurred on the second day of their holiday. Despite efforts by the father and staff to control the horse, India was dragged away and sustained fatal injuries. Police and doctors concluded that the cause of death was head injury and shock. The family, including the child's mother and siblings, had been staying in a five-star hotel in Mumbai before traveling to Matheran. Police stated that the family chose not to press charges, considering it an accident.

Liverpool Mayor Joe Anderson, who was paid £80,000 annually as the council leader, received £4,500 per year from Chesterfield High School, a school he had previously worked at as a mentor. Despite providing no direct services to the school, Anderson made these payments, which were later stopped by the school. When Chesterfield High School discontinued the payments, Anderson sued the school for discrimination, claiming he did so based on his "philosophical belief" in public service. However, a court ruled that the payments were misuse of public funds and that the school was correct to discontinue them, finding that Anderson was not a victim of discrimination.

Jermain Defoe, despite Sunderland's struggles in the Premier League relegation battle with only one win in nine matches, was spotted enjoying a game of basketball during a sunny day. He had scored a goal for the Black Cats in a 1-0 win against Newcastle United earlier. Defoe's team is currently fighting hard for survival, with their next few matches away from home including games against Arsenal and Chelsea, making their situation increasingly precarious.

Sebastien Pocognoli, a left back for West Bromwich Albion, has expressed confusion and frustration after losing his regular spot on the starting lineup. Despite his previous importance to the team under manager Alan Irvine, he now finds his game time limited, even when teammates are injured or suspended. Pocognoli feels uncertain about his future with the club, weighing whether to remain in the Premier League or seek opportunities elsewhere.

Everton manager Roberto Martinez defended Ross Barkley's choice to take the penalty despite Leighton Baines being available, which was similar to a controversial incident last year. This time, Barkley stepped up to take the 10th-minute kick but failed, with Burnley's Tom Heaton saving it. Despite the miss, the decision pleased Martinez, who prefers flexibility in penalty choices based on players' comfort and readiness. Everton ultimately won the match 1-0.

Phil Haste, 60, received a £50 fine from Torbay Council for displaying a valid parking permit but not showing a tax disc, which was no longer required under new government regulations. Despite the tax disc no longer being mandatory since October, Haste insists he has never needed to show it, and disputes why he should be fined for a policy change that took effect over six months ago. He plans to appeal against the fine.

Iker Casillas, despite a clean sheet against Rayo Vallecano in an away game, continues to face intense scrutiny and criticism for his mistakes this season. He keeps the clean sheet against Rayo, and maintains that being dropped from the lineup is not about protecting him, but rather about encouraging winning. Casillas emphasizes that what matters is performing well for the team.

Moeen Ali, an English cricket star, has been appointed as an honorary ambassador for Liverpool's official fan club in Pakistan, known as the Pak Reds. At 27, Ali, a lifelong Liverpool FC fan, will participate in their official matches and events. He joined forces with other notable figures like Aisam-ul-Haq, Noreen Khan, and Dr. Zaf Iqbal to support the club in Pakistan. His appointment underscores his passion for both cricket and football, and it strengthens Liverpool's connection with fans across the country.

In Alderholt, Dorset, an Easter Bunny left chocolate eggs at every home, surprising 3,000 residents with a sweet and unexpected gift. The gesture, which affected every house, was met with delight and widespread social media excitement, as it hadn't occurred in the village for some time. This act of kindness has been praised as a wonderful example of random acts of generosity.

The BBC is airing a two-hour, real-time documentary following a canalboat as it travels at a leisurely 4 mph along the Kennet and Avon Canal. Filmed entirely without narration or music, the documentary captures the calm and scenic beauty of the journey, with only the sounds of the boat's engine, water lapping, and local birds. Despite the lack of excitement, viewers are drawn to the continuous, uninterrupted stream of images, described as a "sensory experience" that allows them to appreciate the changing scenery and slow pace of life on the waterways.

Gareth Bale, injured during Real Madrid's 3-1 victory over Malaga, will miss their upcoming Champions League match against Atletico Madrid. Despite his absence, Bale remains optimistic about his recovery and continues his rehabilitation while encouraging his teammates. Real Madrid head coach Carlo Ancelotti expects to field a strong lineup, including younger players like Isco, Jese, and Hulk, in Bale's absence.

A massive wildfire erupted in Southern California due to a cooking stove accident, forcing over 300 homes to evacuate in the early hours of the night. The fire, initially containing 35% of its size, grew rapidly to cover an area of 1.6 square miles, threatening numerous homes between the cities of Norco and Corona. Despite being fiercely fought off by firefighters, no property damage or injuries were reported. Evacuation orders were lifted just before dawn, allowing affected residents to return home.

Charlene Mess, 48, was accused of murdering her 52-year-old husband at their farm in New York. Her son reported his father missing, leading to an extensive search. Mess was eventually found hiding her husband's body under a pile of manure at their rural 300-acre farm. She was charged with second-degree murder and remains in custody.

During the Chinese Grand Prix, Jenson Button crashed into Pastor Maldonado, resulting in Button being penalized with a five-second time penalty and two points added to his superlicence. This incident occurred during a race where Maldonado had already experienced several problems, and it further tarnished McLaren's performance, with Button finishing 14th and Alonso 12th.

The lawyers for the parents of Michael Brown, the unarmed teenager shot by a police officer, have announced plans to file a civil lawsuit against Ferguson city. The lawsuit, which had been anticipated, will name former Officer Darren Wilson and is expected to be filed shortly, continuing the legal process begun after Brown's death in 2014. This follows the national "Black Lives Matter" movement demanding changes in police interactions with minorities.

Chris Smalling, a key defender for Manchester United, recently joined Twitter ahead of an important match against Manchester City. The 25-year-old defender, who plays a crucial role in United's defense, has established himself as one of the best defenders in England. Meanwhile, United teammate Adnan Januzaj also joined Twitter, indicating he may seek more playing time or even potentially leave the club in the summer. Manchester United is looking to strengthen their position for the upcoming Champions League, while Manchester City faces challenges after their recent loss to Crystal Palace.

Sergio Barrientos-Hinojosa, an inebriated Albuquerque man, shot at another customer at a gas station after an argument. He used his 13-year-old son as a getaway driver, but the boy was able to escape. Barrientos-Hinojosa was eventually arrested, and the incident ended when police found the gun he had thrown moments earlier. The teenager, who was unharmed, was taken into custody. The case against Barrientos-Hinojosa includes charges of child abuse.

In response to a state-wide ban on beef, police in Malegaon, India, are requiring residents to submit photos and detailed information about their cows as part of an effort to prevent the illegal slaughter and consumption of beef. This new policy comes after a recent case of cow slaughter and involves photographing and identifying the animals to help track down those involved. It reflects the strong cultural and religious significance of cows, particularly in Maharashtra, where Hinduism is prevalent. Despite some controversy, including a beef traders' strike, the measure aims to enforce existing laws and protect the status of cows in Indian culture.

In a goalless draw at Staines Town, Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur both missed out on a potential opportunity to reduce their gap to Manchester United in the Under 21 league. Despite both sides creating several chances, including a late goal-scoring chance for Chelsea, neither managed to secure all three points. Spurs maintained their third-place position, just behind Manchester United and Liverpool, while Chelsea stayed in eighth place. Notably, Tottenham's performance included the promising displays of defender DeAndre Yedlin and midfielder Ruben Loftus-Cheek, who was forced off due to injury early in the match.

The Yichuan Senior High School in Shaanxi province held its first outdoor exam for freshmen students due to the lack of indoor space for so many students to take the exam simultaneously. Over 1,700 students took the exam in the school's playground, with students suggesting and organizing the outdoor setting themselves. They rotated their seats periodically to avoid sun glare and some believed it could prevent cheating. The school noted this event could test the students' organizing skills.

The Coast Guard cutter arrived in San Diego with 14 tons of cocaine, part of a larger seizure operation involving U.S. and Canadian forces in the eastern Pacific Ocean. This operation includes 19 separate seizures of cocaine, with a total of over 28 tons valued at $848 million, marking the most successful period for drug seizures in that region since 2009. Multiple ships and aircraft tracked suspicious vessels, and crews boarded them to intercept and seize the drugs.

Jose Mourinho praises Eden Hazard, comparing him to Messi and Ronaldo as one of the best players in the world. Despite being young, Hazard is bearing the weight of being a star player and has scored crucial goals for Chelsea. The Belgian winger attributes his success to understanding his role and managing both strong and weak moments in games, and he is considered a strong contender for the PFA Player of the Year award.

Franz Beckenbauer, a former Bayern Munich legend, believes that Jurgen Klopp could potentially succeed Pep Guardiola at Bayern Munich. Klopp previously led Borussia Dortmund to two Bundesliga titles in seven seasons, and Beckenbauer has expressed his belief that Klopp would be a good fit at Bayern given their history together. However, Beckenbauer also noted that it remains to be seen whether Guardiola will stay at Bayern for the full contract period. Additionally, Beckenbauer emphasized the importance of having a complete team featuring players like Franck Ribéry and Arjen Robben to compete effectively against Barcelona in the Champions League.

Esteban Cambiasso, who has won multiple domestic titles and the Champions League, recently joined Leicester City to prevent the English Premier League strugglers from relegating to the Championship. Despite the challenging circumstances, Cambiasso believes saving Leicester from relegation would be as significant as winning trophies with other teams due to the immense effort required. Leicester, currently in the relegation zone, faces a tough task to stay in the Premier League. Cambiasso's experience could be crucial in helping his new team avoid returning to the second tier.

Jonathan Trott made his first Test appearance in 17 months, but his debut was short-lived. Caught at first slip for only three balls, he returned a duck. The 35-year-old batsman, who last played for England in 2013, was initially given more responsibility as the top-order opener but struggled. His dismissal came early, and England were all out for one run in their first innings against the West Indies.

Shannon Hayes, a 12-year-old schoolgirl, claims to have laid the smallest chicken egg ever, measuring only 1.8 cm in length, which is about the size of a 5p coin. This egg is 2 mm smaller than the previous record holder's egg, which was 2.1 cm long. Shannon discovered the tiny egg among her hens' normal-sized eggs and took it to be measured to verify her claim. Currently, it is stored in the fridge for further testing.

Six Leeds United players withdrew from a scheduled match against Charlton due to injuries, but head coach Neil Redfearn and the club's medical staff were unaware of their conditions. The withdrawn players include several signed by banned president Massimo Cellino, and their absence has drawn criticism from fans, highlighting ongoing issues within the club. Head coach Redfearn's future at the club remains uncertain, as he faces increasing pressure amid recent managerial changes and financial troubles.

Nicola Sturgeon, the leader of the Scottish National Party, has undergone a significant style transformation since the 2007 Scottish Parliament election. Now appearing more polished and fashionable, she wore a vibrant fuchsia column dress to support the BBC's Andrew Marr Show, showcasing her new look with a slimmer physique. Her recent style changes, including a personal shopper, stylist, and updated wardrobe, have been noted, shifting her image significantly from a more traditional and less glamorous appearance.

John R. Lind, a hardware store worker, was found guilty of intentionally ejaculating into his female coworker Pat Maahs' coffee in an attempt to draw attention. This incident initially went unnoticed, but when Maahs smelled something wrong with her milk, she discovered Lind's actions. He was convicted of indecent exposure and faces sentencing on May 22. As a result, Maahs proposed making such behavior a felony, indicating her strong desire for stronger penalties.

According to Jamie Carragher, Chelsea captain John Terry is the best defender to have played in the Premier League. Despite being 34 years old, Terry had an exceptional season, leading Chelsea to a 0-0 draw against Arsenal. His performance against Arsenal was so impressive that Chelsea manager Jose Mourinho called it his captain's best performance in a Chelsea shirt. Terry's influence extends beyond defense, making him a key player in Chelsea's success, as highlighted by both Carragher and Mourinho.

Detectives investigating the suspected murder of Deborah Steel, who vanished in 1997, have dug up the patio of the Royal Standard pub where she worked. Despite efforts, the 37-year-old's body has not been found, and police have recently reclassified the case as a murder inquiry. The search has the full consent of the current pub landlord, who is unconnected to the investigation.

Manny Pacquiao, preparing for his historic $300 million fight against Floyd Mayweather, has called the bout "the last fight of my life" if he wants to win. Despite being an underdog, Pacquiao insists he must embody a warrior spirit, fighting each round as if it were the final one, to triumph over the undefeated Mayweather. His goal is to make Mayweather lose, and he promises to capitalize on any chance to knockout his opponent.

A graffiti artist tagged a police horse named Charly with the letters "RBS" and an arrow during patrol in Venice Beach. Despite the animal rights concerns, the LAPD hopes to find the vandal through the tag "RBS." The horse was quickly cleaned, and the police have asked for public assistance to identify the person responsible.

A 13-year-old boy allegedly embezzled a large sum of $25,000 from his grandfather's bedside table and distributed the money among his school friends. He then spent $7,000 of the cash and purchased items for himself and others during a week-long spree at a local middle school. Local authorities are investigating, and some of the stolen money and items have been recovered, though charges have not yet been filed due to ongoing investigations.

Bastia president Pierre-Marie Geronimi demanded that French League President Frederic Thiriez resign after Thiriez failed to meet with the Bastia team before the League Cup final, which ended in a 4-0 loss to Paris Saint-Germain. This incident followed Thiriez's previous refusal to present the Ligue 2 trophy to Bastia in 2012.

In an April Fools' prank gone wrong, Hayleigh McBay, 17, from Elgin, Scotland, pretended to dump her boyfriend David Clarke via WhatsApp at midnight. However, the joke backfired when David also feigned wanting to end their relationship. The prank went viral, with the couple's playful exchange retweeted over 12,000 times. Despite Hayleigh clarifying they had no prior plan, the situation led David to joke about being "so disappointed" if his mother found out he was joking about being pregnant.

David Wihby, 61, a senior aide to US Senator Kelly Ayotte and her state director for a year, was arrested for allegedly soliciting a prostitute and subsequently resigned. The incident occurred when Wihby was caught engaging in prostitution; he was later released on bail and will face charges in May. This arrest came amid other similar arrests made in Nashua over the past week, involving ten men charged with solicitation. Senator Ayotte described Wihby as her longtime friend, but expressed shock at the situation.

Arsene Wenger reveals he makes his final decision on the starting lineup just before the match, often agonizing over choices throughout the week. He bases his selections on intuition, experience, and the balance between team members, and consults with medical staff. Wenger’s extensive experience, spanning 1,056 matches, has led him to three Premier League titles and five FA Cups.

Tom, a Freemason contestant on Jeopardy, gave an inappropriate answer to a question about puberty by saying "What is the age of consent?" instead of the correct answer "What is puberty?". This led to widespread controversy and embarrassment, as many viewers felt the answer implied inappropriate adult content. The question, which should have been about the onset of puberty, was misinterpreted by the contestant, causing significant public outcry and trended on Twitter.

Rafael Nadal secured a straight-set win (6-2, 6-1) over Lucas Pouille in the first round of the Monte Carlo Masters. This victory marks the Spanish star's perfect start to his clay court season and boosts his confidence after recent disappointments. Despite being an eight-time champion at this event, Nadal is playing cautiously, focusing on building his self-belief after some recent losses.

Auckland coastguards were surprised by a homemade boat race that featured unconventional vessels like a couch strapped to tyres, a floating Subaru, and other DIY boats. This annual fishing event saw about 5 homemade 'boats' participate in a local event near Gulf Harbour. Despite a capsized vessel causing minor distress, no injuries occurred due to the volunteers using life jackets and monitoring weather conditions. The event, which has been going on for years, aims to bring positivity to the community.

Navinder Sarao, a British trader, allegedly boasted that he could make £1 million in a week during volatile markets. This prediction preceded the "Flash Crash" of Wall Street on May 6, 2010, which saw a sudden and sharp drop in stock prices. Sarao is alleged to have used this crash to profit $27 million, and U.S. prosecutors believe he triggered the event from his home in London. Sarao was arrested last week and remains in custody.

An official advert for the Mayweather vs Pacquiao fight was recently released. Filmed in a Los Angeles studio, the 30-second clip features both fighters walking towards each other and setting up for their upcoming pay-per-view event in Las Vegas. The ad emphasizes the significance of the bout, which is expected to set new records for pay-per-view sales, potentially breaking the $102 million record set by Mayweather's fight with Saul 'Canelo' Alvarez in 2013.

Rory McIlroy promoted a new football game called Circular Soccer by defeating actor Jamie Dornan 2-1 in a match. Circular Soccer features a three-sided goal designed to enhance players' ball control and skill. Despite being the world's top-ranked golfer, McIlroy chose to focus on this new sport, having previously competed in the Masters tournament. The game, developed by Dornan's father, has received support from FIFA and Manchester United, and its potential for widespread play suggests it might become popular in parks across the globe.

Colleen Ann Harris, a 73-year-old grandmother, was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2013 shooting death of her third husband, Robert Harris, who had been found dead from a shotgun blast. This conviction followed a 30-year delay; previously, Harris had been acquitted of killing her second husband in 1985. During the trial, Harris wept as the jury returned a guilty verdict. Her sentencing will take place on June 5, where it is expected that she will face 50 years to life in prison. This case highlights the possibility of legal accountability over multiple decades, especially in cases involving domestic violence.

Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao, who are considered the best fighters in the world, have been spending quality time with their loved ones leading up to their historic $165 million fight. Mayweather has been with his girlfriend Doralie Medina, while Pacquiao was joined by his wife Jinkee and their five children. The fighters will soon begin their final weeks of intense training to reach their competition weight limit of 154 lbs.

Martin Guptill has been recalled to New Zealand's Test squad for their upcoming two-match series against England. This marks Guptill's return to Test cricket since 2013, following a successful innings of 237 against the West Indies in the World Cup. Additionally, paceman Matt Henry has been called up to the Test squad for the first time, despite playing several ODIs and one-day internationals.

Andrew Harrison of Kentucky uttered a racial slur towards Frank Kaminsky of Wisconsin during a postgame press conference following the NCAA Final Four game. After the incident, Harrison apologized for his offensive language, stating it was meant in jest and he regretted its unintended impact. Harrison's comment brought attention to the situation, but the two players managed to resolve the issue amicably, and Harrison expressed confidence in their future success.

Scottish comedian Limmy, known for his show "Limmy's Show," shared a disturbing video on social media showing a three-inch house spider crawling out of his mouth and across his face. Despite the revolting sight, he humorously captioned it with "Sweet dreams." The video sparked both shock and intrigue among viewers, leading to various reactions ranging from disgust to amusement.

Andras Janos Vass, a Hungarian man, was convicted of human trafficking and racketeering for recruiting young, gay men from Eastern Europe to the United States, where they were kept captive and forced into prostitution. He lured potential victims through a dating site, took their travel documents, and made them work long hours in sex work under threat of violence against their families. Vass faced up to 155 years in prison and will serve at least 21 years behind bars. Three victims testified against him during his trial.

Chinese tourists traveling internationally have significantly increased, leading Beijing International Airport to construct the world's largest terminal, Terminal 1, set to open in 2018. This massive facility, covering 700,000 square meters and expected to handle 45 million passengers annually, features a six-tier concept designed by renowned architect Zaha Hadid. The terminal aims to reduce customer walking distances and enhance connectivity, symbolizing a modern and welcoming experience for global travelers.

On Thursday, the famous Icebergs Club pool in Bondi, Sydney, was unexpectedly drained to serve as a unique catwalk for the Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia event. Models walked through the now-empty pool while spectators gazed at the ocean backdrop, creating a memorable and unconventional fashion presentation. Despite the unusual setting, the show received positive reception, showcasing Ten Pieces' ten-piece capsule collection designed to be versatile and worn by both sexes.

Alain Robert, known as the "French Spiderman," accomplished the daring feat of climbing Dubai's 1,007-foot Cayan Tower without a safety harness, using only sticky tape and chalk for assistance. This 75-story skyscraper features a unique 90-degree twist. The 52-year-old climber scaled the towering structure, which had hundreds of spectators watching, in an hour and a half. While previously he has faced legal issues for similar climbs, this particular attempt was under the supervision of event organizers.

Stuart Pearce, former Manchester City player and manager, argues that Manuel Pellegrini deserves more time to revive Manchester City's fortunes. Despite the team's recent struggles, including a disappointing loss against Manchester United and their current lackluster performance in the league, Pearce believes in giving Pellegrini more patience. However, he acknowledges that the club will likely assess its situation in the summer and may consider changing management. Pearce points out that City's problems often coincide with Yaya Toure's reduced form, which suggests potential issues with the squad.

A married couple, Derek Lowe and Tina Lowe, was killed when an Amtrak train hit them on tracks in Durham, North Carolina on Easter Sunday. The train, the Carolinian, was traveling from Charlotte to New York City when the accident occurred. No other passengers on the train were hurt, but one man who was delayed briefly received medical attention. Authorities suggest the couple may have been living in a homeless camp, which they often collected scrap metal from. The train was delayed for about three hours after the incident.

Paul Gregory, 51, from Bedford, was caught using a blue badge improperly, as he claimed to be unable to walk more than 200 meters due to exhaustion, even though he was a keen walker and a member of a mountaineering club. This deception came to light when authorities discovered a newspaper article about his long-distance hiking trip. After pleading guilty, Gregory was fined £795, ordered to do 150 hours of unpaid work, and given a supervision order. The incident exemplified one of the most outrageous cases of blue badge fraud the Bedford Borough Council had investigated.

Manchester United have expressed concerns about the possibility of pursuing Hugo Lloris due to Tottenham Hotspur's reported plan to demand a £35 million transfer fee. This high valuation, reflecting Tottenham's desire to retain their key player, could make any potential move very challenging, especially given that the French goalkeeper is considered one of the Premier League's top keepers. Consequently, United are likely to reconsider such a move, especially with David De Gea's uncertain future.

This year, the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) experienced significant issues due to budget cuts, resulting in an overloaded phone system that disconnected more than eight million taxpayers during tax-filing season. Only 40% of those who called received human assistance, with many waiting on hold for over 30 minutes. IRS Commissioner John Koskinen blamed these problems on funding cuts passed by Congress, highlighting the challenges the agency faces in providing adequate services while implementing the Affordable Care Act.

Jeremy Clarkson, after being fired from the BBC for alleged assault, participated in a charity auction while jokingly discussing his past career at the BBC. He claimed that being fired allowed him to now swear freely without fear of reprimand. This came during a special appearance where he opened the event and interacted with fans, using his usual provocative humor.

Undercover police report that Chilean pickpockets are the most effective thieves operating in central London. These groups of up to six members follow wealthy shoppers in the West End, primarily targeting tourists from Arab countries. The gangs are known for their intelligence and meticulous approach, operating in mixed teams of three to six individuals who are often middle-aged. They use sophisticated tactics and have a high success rate, making them a significant threat in London's busy shopping areas.

An accidental vehicle fire occurred at an apartment complex in Las Vegas, killing two children while two others survived but were found dead inside the burning van. The incident happened when one of the children accidentally ignited the van, which then exploded, trapping the other two children inside. Authorities believe the incident was accidental and due to the young age of the child who started the fire, with no criminal charges expected.

Real Madrid's players were warmly greeted by excited supporters upon arriving at the Santiago Bernabéu for their Champions League match against Atletico Madrid. The crowd's enthusiastic welcome included lighting flares and banging on the windows, creating a chaotic atmosphere that even rival supporters felt. Javier Hernandez scored the decisive goal for Real Madrid in the 88th minute, securing a 1-0 aggregate victory over Atletico, which gave them a significant advantage in the semi-final draw.

On NBC's Today show, a graphics error mistakenly identified Saturday Night Live creator Lorne Michaels as "Lauren." This oversight, initially noticed during broadcasts in the Eastern time zone, was quickly corrected in other regions and online. Lorne Michaels, now 70 years old, is a legendary figure at NBC and has created numerous comedy careers through the iconic sketch show, Saturday Night Live. Despite this embarrassing mistake, Michaels remains a highly influential comedy producer.

In a tragic incident during a Korean Union United Methodist Church service in New Jersey, the ceiling suddenly collapsed, injuring 14 people. With 75 individuals present, thirteen had minor injuries and one suffered a serious head injury. The event occurred mid-song, as observed by witnesses. Chaos ensued, with debris raining down on churchgoers, leading to multiple cuts and bruises. The cause of the collapse is under investigation, and the church remains closed pending safety inspections.

German Chancellor Angela Merkel spent this year's Easter holiday on the island of Ischia, where she was disappointed by the cold weather, settling for an average temperature of 12 degrees Celsius. Despite being traditionally known for their luxurious Mediterranean getaway, the couple, including Chancellor Merkel's husband, Professor Joachim Sauer, chose to enjoy less sunny conditions and focused on relaxation. They engaged in activities such as Pilates classes and healthy vegetarian meals, and even hired a personal Pilates trainer. Meanwhile, Professor Sauer prefers a more low-key approach, avoiding public appearances.

Radamel Falamel of Manchester United believes Sergio Aguero from Manchester City is the biggest threat to his team in this weekend's Manchester Derby. This view comes from Falamel, who spoke at a Puma event alongside Aguero. Despite not playing in the previous Manchester Derby, Falamel expressed excitement about participating in the upcoming match. Falamel also stated his satisfaction at Manchester United, expressing appreciation for the club and patience under manager Louis van Gaal.

Andy Murray, having just married his childhood sweetheart Kim Sears, celebrated another win for Barcelona by attending their Champions League match against Paris Saint-Germain. This marked Murray's second visit to the Nou Camp within a week, this time watching Lionel Messi lead the team to a 2-0 victory. The British tennis player was there in the company of his best man, Ross Hutchins. Murray plans to participate in the BMW Open in Munich later and return to Spain for the Madrid Masters.

Martina Navratilova, the former Grand Slam champion and now a tennis coach, ended her short collaboration with Agnieszka Radwanska, who recently lost in the first round of the Stuttgart tournament. Their partnership began in December but was deemed unsuccessful due to Radwanska's poor form and Navratilova's inability to fully commit. The French Open is scheduled to start on May 24, and both players expressed mutual disappointment about the coaching relationship.

On St George's Day, many English and former English footballers used social media to celebrate the occasion, sharing photos and videos. Harry Kane shared a picture of him as a young boy with his brother, while Rio Ferdinand posted an image from his time in the England squad. Gary Cahill created a video for England Rugby, while other players like Nathaniel Clyne, Charlie Austin, and Peter Crouch also wished followers a happy St George's Day.

Cristiano Ronaldo achieved a remarkable feat, scoring his 300th goal for Real Madrid in just 288 appearances, marking an impressive milestone. The 30-year-old reached this milestone on April 8 against Rayo Vallecano, surpassing several legendary players like Raul and Alfredo di Stefano. To celebrate this achievement, Ronaldo was given a special '300' shirt by the club president Florentino Pérez. Ronaldo's rapid rise to such a high goal-scoring average shows his enduring impact on Real Madrid and the Spanish league.

Assistant coach Fernando Hierro, who previously played for Real Madrid, is confident that the current squad can win a second consecutive Champions League, citing the club's rich history and strong current roster. Hierro believes Real Madrid's DNA includes the drive to be European champions, and he sees this as a real possibility given the strength of the team and Carlo Ancelotti's leadership.

Brazilian forward Kenedy is a favorite to join Chelsea, who have reportedly reached an agreement with his agent, Giuliano Bertolucci. His representation by former Chelsea player Deco adds to Chelsea's interest in him. Additionally, Kenedy has been linked with other major European clubs like Manchester United and Barcelona, but Chelsea appears to be in a strong position to secure his signing.

Mario Mandzukic, who suffered multiple injuries including a cut to his nose and a stomach punch from Real Madrid defenders, will be available for Atletico Madrid's Champions League return match against Real Madrid on Wednesday. Despite missing the first leg due to the injuries, Mandzukic is recovering and in training, and is expected to play as Atletico looks to overcome Real Madrid in the second leg. Mandzukic and Atletico have been dominant in La Liga, with Mandzukic scoring 20 goals so far this season.

Italian authorities have launched a significant anti-terrorism operation, arresting 18 suspects linked to al-Qaeda planning attacks in Italy, Afghanistan, and Pakistan. Some of the suspects had direct communication with Osama bin Laden prior to his death, and the group considered the Vatican as one of their potential targets. The operation, involving wiretap evidence, has uncovered extensive plans for both domestic and international terrorism and has involved years of surveillance.

Real Madrid are interested in signing Manchester United's David De Gea, but want a "reasonable" price and are prepared to wait until next summer if De Gea refuses negotiations. De Gea has had a great season, and Casillas remains unreliable. Meanwhile, Real Madrid may buy Petr Cech from Chelsea if De Gea leaves. De Gea’s contract ends next year, and United is unsure about his future. David De Gea's injury recovery status is reported to be positive by Sport, though it hasn't affected his return to action for Real Madrid. Paul Dybala's injury continues, though Marca notes he could still play for Inter Milan next season.

Ben Flower returned to the field after a six-month suspension following his controversial punch in the Grand Final against St Helens. Despite this, he received a standing ovation from the crowd, expressing surprise but gratitude. Coach Shaun Wane had hoped for a more assertive Flower, but the player emphasized that aggression should not equate to dirty play, and he recognizes his failure to control emotions adequately.

Wildlife-rescue volunteer Sandie Konopelski, 58, was struck and killed by a MetroLink train near an Illinois train station after responding to a call about an opossum on the tracks. She had been struck by a westbound commuter train while responding to the opossum alert. The cause of her death is unclear, but authorities do not believe she heard the train approaching. Konopelski was known for her dedication to animal rescue work.

A Pennsylvania man named Kevin Rebbie, 56, has been arrested and charged with multiple offenses related to sexually abusing a 15-year-old girl over a four-year period. Surveillance footage of the girl and others was discovered in a hidden camera in the girl's bathroom. The man is accused of recording the girl while she was undressing and showering, and the videos included graphic content showing the victims' genitals. Rebbie faces charges including aggravated indecent assault, unlawful contact with a minor, and invasion of privacy.

Aaronessa Keaton, who was eight months pregnant and under the influence of drugs, was involved in a head-on collision that killed a 5-year-old child who was in the other car. Keaton initially showed no remorse for her actions, saying "Sh*t happens" upon being informed of the child's death. She was subsequently arrested, charged with manslaughter, and taken into custody after being pulled over for a traffic violation. Her baby, also exposed to the same substances, was critically injured and required an emergency C-section delivery.

Football Association chairman Greg Dyke returned a £16,000 watch he received as a gift from the Brazilian Football Association (CBF). The watch had been part of 65 other limited-edition Parmigiani watches given to attendees at the FIFA Congress during the World Cup. Dyke initially refused to return the watch due to a promise to donate it to the FA's charity partner, Breast Cancer Care, but ultimately complied with FIFA's demands after facing potential sanctions. Now, all the watches will be auctioned for a Brazilian charity, with Dyke also making a personal donation to Breast Cancer Care.

Nedum Onuoha, a 28-year-old QPR center-back with a contract until January, is reportedly among several QPR players looking to leave the club due to potential squad reductions. He recently made his first start for the team in a key victory against West Bromwich Albion, and is being targeted by Hull City, Everton, and West Ham United. Additionally, QPR are considering moving for Catania defender Nicolas Spolli, while West Bromwich Albion striker Victor Anichebe is also attracting interest from Hull City.

The documentary film "Kid Kulafu" chronicles the journey of Manny Pacquiao from his humble beginnings in the Philippines to his rise to fame, culminating in his upcoming fight against Floyd Mayweather. Directed by Paul Soriano and based on personal conversations with Pacquiao, the film captures his growth and the challenges he faced, emphasizing his resilience and faith. The film has premiered in Manila and will soon be released in the United States and Canada, leading up to Pacquiao's historic match against Mayweather.

Floyd Mayweather Jr. has been seen swimming in a pool as part of his intense training regimen leading up to his big fight with Manny Pacquiao. The 38-year-old boxer completed several laps in the pool while his trainer watched. Both fighters are nearing their epic showdown in Las Vegas, which is expected to be one of the most lucrative fights in boxing history. The event is anticipated to exceed both the pay-per-view records of previous major bouts.

Benedict Cumberbatch, known for his role as Sherlock Holmes, was challenged to a table tennis match by Chinese ping pong legend Deng Yaping at the Laureus World Sports Awards in Shanghai. Despite using a wooden spoon to even the playing field, Deng ultimately defeated Cumberbatch, much to the comedian’s surprise. The encounter went viral, with Cumberbatch jokingly referring to it as "my mother used to say to me."

Manchester City's top stars risk missing out on significant bonuses if they don't qualify for the Champions League this season due to poor recent form and transfers. Players like Sergio Aguero, David Silva, Joe Hart, and Yaya Toure are incentivized under the club's financial fair play rules to ensure they stay within UEFA regulations. Manuel Pellegrini's team currently sit in fourth place and face competition from rivals such as Manchester United, Liverpool, and Paul Pogba, who United also want to sign.

The article discusses CNN Student News' broadcast on April 8, 2015, which touched on several topics including California's ongoing drought, a flu outbreak in Chicago (affecting animals), and a proposed undersea tunnel connecting Denmark and Germany. Additionally, the article mentions the CNN Student News' show transcript and its request form for students to request to be included on the roll call segment.

In Hong Kong, there's a thriving black market for a popular local delicacy, Jenny's cookies, which are sold for $9 per tin. This trend has led to the creation of knockoff versions, and the original store warns against buying 'fake' cookies. The intense demand has caused long queues, with some people even hiring others to stand in line to resell them for a higher price. While the cookie craze is not unique to Hong Kong, similar food crazes have swept other parts of the world, such as cronuts and ramen burgers, though their lasting impact remains questionable.

Kevin Coulton, a 16-year-old schoolboy from Manchester, accidentally injured himself while trying the 'Kylie Jenner' challenge, where participants suck their mouths into a glass to achieve a fuller pout. His attempt left him with dark bruising that lasted over three days. As a result, he is now warning his classmates against participating in the trend, as it has caused harm to many other young people who have tried it. The challenge has become popular among teenagers seeking to imitate Kylie Jenner's appearance but has resulted in unwanted bruising and swelling.

Bristol City faces Bradford City on Tuesday to secure promotion from League One. Despite having won their previous match against Preston, Bristol City's fate still rested on results outside their control. The Robins maintain an eight-point advantage over Preston, who must wait for other matches' results. Manager Steve Cotterill is focused on preparing his team thoroughly for the upcoming match, maintaining their pursuit of the league title.

President Obama reaffirmed his commitment to ensuring Israel maintains a military advantage over Iran. This comes amidst criticism from Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, who is concerned about the deal with Iran and the potential loss of Israel's qualitative military edge. Obama argues that the agreement will provide clarity and deterrence against any potential future attacks, even if Iran cannot be fully trusted.

Chris Christie appeared on The Tonight Show and criticized Jimmy Fallon's weight jokes about him. When Fallon said Christie "looks great," Christie jokingly questioned why there were still jokes about him. To make amends, Fallon offered him a special ice cream treat, but Christie refused to share it. Christie confirmed he hasn't decided whether to run for president, but stressed he will not change his identity to run.

Manchester City's Under 18s team lost 3-1 to Chelsea in the FA Youth Cup final first-leg. Despite criticism of the club for lacking young talent, the team was cheered by Manchester City captain Vincent Kompany, manager Manuel Pellegrini, and head of youth development Patrick Vieira. The loss saw Chelsea in the lead, while City's Isaac Buckley scored to level the score briefly. Dominic Solanke's late winner secured the victory for Chelsea, who are currently top of the Premier League, while Manchester City's under-23 team is fourth in the league.

In an unusual drug trafficking attempt, a suspect used an Easter bunny to hide $30,000 worth of methamphetamine. The package was intercepted by police after it was sent to a known buyer, Carolyn Ross. Upon inspection, two condom-like bags containing the drugs were found inside the toy. Ross has now been arrested and faces charges related to drug trafficking.

Andre Ayew, a 25-year-old striker from Marseille who has scored for Ghana in the World Cup, is out of contract this summer and is attracting interest from multiple clubs, including Swansea City, Newcastle, and Everton. Additionally, Schalke full-back Christian Fuchs, also interested in a move to the Premier League, is currently considering offers from Sunderland, Hull, and Fulham, with Schalke hoping to reach an agreement before the end of the season. Swansea is watching Fuchs closely as they look to replace their left-back Neil Taylor.

Jack Wilshere thanked his Arsenal U21 supporters for attending the team's match against Reading, which he captained due to a recent injury. Despite a goal from Niall Keown, the under-21s lost the game. Wilshere expressed gratitude for the home crowd and hopes to make it back into the Arsenal first team for the season's remaining matches, including the FA Cup semi-final against Reading.

Vincent Kompany, now a Manchester City captain, revealed that his mother prevented him from joining Manchester United as a 17-year-old. Initially, Ferguson sought to sign Kompany, but his mother insisted he complete his education before pursuing a career in football. Despite the delay, Kompany now expresses gratitude for his time at City, stating it "has played out perfectly," and will lead them against their former club United in the upcoming Derby.

Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao are each within the required weight limit of 147 pounds for their upcoming bout, with Mayweather being just three and a half pounds under the 10% weight limit. The fighters are preparing rigorously for the "Fight of the Century," including using advanced recovery methods like cryotherapy after workouts. Their final weigh-ins are scheduled seven days before the fight, ensuring they stay within the legal weight limits to minimize risks associated with significant pre-fight weight loss.

The world's largest venomous snake, the four-meter long king cobra Raja, has delivered nearly 500 milligrams of lethal venom to veteran handler Billy Collett at Gosford's Australian Reptile Park in New South Wales, Australia. This venom, sufficient to fill a shot glass, was collected despite Raja holding on for minutes after its initial bite. Mr. Collett, who has worked with other venomous snakes for over ten years, described it as "a very dangerous yield." The collected venom will be shared among research institutions across the country.

Joe Root, impressing with a 182-run innings, had his double-century dream snatched away when James Anderson ran him out due to a costly mistake, resulting in Root throwing down his helmet and gloves in frustration. Despite their disagreement, the duo reconciled later, allowing England to maintain a 165-run lead over West Indies entering the second innings.

An old hoax video showing a wave rider surfing in front of the Sydney Opera House has gained new attention after being posted again. This time, it went viral due to recent heavy storms in Sydney. Initially uploaded by a local Sydney radio host, the video has since been widely shared but contradicted by wave experts, who argue that the waves visible in the footage could not exist given the distance from the ocean and the strength of ocean swells.

Manny Pacquiao trained on the streets of Los Angeles at Griffith Park, where he worked on his fitness, stamina, and skill in preparation for his high-profile fight against Floyd Mayweather. He was joined by fitness enthusiasts and his pet dog Pacman during his outdoor workout routine. Pacquiao showcased his impressive core strength and footwork while wearing a colorful mouthguard, which he planned to use in their Las Vegas showdown on May 2nd.

In a front room in North Carolina, three dogs – a Husky named Sky, a brown and white Springer Spaniel called Sadie, and a black and white Springer Spaniel named Marshall – formed a musical ensemble for their human owner. They began with individual barks and soon joined in a harmonious howling that culminated in a synchronized, loud vocal performance directed towards the ceiling. Despite the owner's request for quiet, the dogs continued the performance, eventually turning to look at their owner in surprise.

West Ham United is considering signing Javier Hernández, who scored a crucial goal for Real Madrid in the Champions League. The striker, currently on loan to Real Madrid with Manchester United, will return to Old Trafford at the end of the season. His potential move to West Ham would cost around £7 million, with the club also eyeing other young transfers for their upcoming Premier League season.

A coach caught fire on a busy interchange at the M25 motorway near Dartford in Kent, sending flames into the air and causing a significant disruption. The fire completely destroyed the vehicle but thankfully no one was injured, as only the driver was present. Firefighters battled the fire for an hour, closing the slip road temporarily. The site remains closed, and drivers are advised to avoid the area to avoid traffic congestion. Another incident involving a separate crash near the Dartford Tunnel Tunnel has also affected traffic flow on the M25.

Grupo OAS, which owns Brazil's World Cup venues Dunas Arena in Natal and Fonte Nova in Salvador, is selling these properties due to financial difficulties stemming from a corruption scandal involving Petrobras. The company has also been impacted by a broader economic downturn, government austerity measures, and a weakening currency. This is part of a trend where post-World Cup stadiums have struggled to attract large crowds for other events.

In a controversy involving Lord Janner, Alison Saunders, the Director of Public Prosecutions, decided not to prosecute him despite allegations of child abuse. It was later revealed that her legal advisor, Neil Moore, was also a colleague of Lord Janner’s son. This decision faced significant criticism from various quarters, including Conservative politicians and Labour MPs, leading to calls for an independent review and questioning the integrity of the decision-making process.

Charlie Austin, who has scored 17 goals for QPR despite their relegation struggles, is under consideration for a potential England call-up due to Harry Kane's involvement with the Under-21 team. This decision comes as England's head coach, Roy Hodgson, looks for additional forwards for their upcoming summer fixtures, including a friendly with Ireland and a Euro 2016 qualifier against Slovenia. Although Austin's club is fighting relegation, Hodgson has shown interest in him due to his consistency and potential impact.

The return of "Game of Thrones" has led to a significant increase in online piracy, with fans downloading over 100,000 illegal copies daily. Over seven million episodes from seasons one to four were illegally downloaded between February and April, showing a 45% year-over-year rise compared to the same period in 2014. Despite efforts by HBO, including an early release in 170 countries and a new streaming service, piracy remains a major issue, with the show continuing to be among the most illegally downloaded series worldwide.

Former Manchester United defender Patrice Evra, now at Juventus, recounted a significant moment with Sir Alex Ferguson when the legendary coach told him he would be a great coach one day, along with Ryan Giggs. Evra, who spent eight years at Manchester United and won five Premier League titles, claims Ferguson also shared his belief that Evra had the potential to become a successful manager. Evra has since moved to Juventus and continues to pursue his coaching qualifications, including passing some coaching badges while still at United.

A breakthrough in understanding noise-induced hearing loss has been made by researchers at the University of Southern California who identified a gene, Nox3, located in the inner ear that determines a person's vulnerability to hearing loss from loud noise. This finding is significant because it could allow individuals at higher genetic risk to proactively protect their hearing before being exposed to hazardous noise environments. Previous gene association studies had limited scope and replication.

Susie Clark, an Arkansas resident, discovered a 3.69-carat white diamond while visiting Crater of Diamonds State Park. This find made her the newest member of the park's "diamond-hunters club," having discovered 122 diamonds so far in the park this year. Clark named the gem "Hallelujah Diamond" after praying for a find during her visit. Her discovery is notable not only for its size but also for the recent heavy rainfall, which typically brings diamonds to the surface, increasing visitors' chances of finding them.

Magistrate Michael Dakin denied a request by O'Connor's lawyer for the media to be banned from naming the former child safety head due to the risk of causing undue stress and embarrassment. While the media were allowed to cover the case, sensitive information about the victims and witnesses was suppressed. O'Connor, charged with multiple child sex offenses, faces trial at the Campbelltown court.

David Seaman, now 51, recalls making the FA Cup-winning save against Sheffield United in 2008, which remains his best save during his career according to him. This save occurred during Arsenal's 1,000th competitive appearance for both club and country, marking a significant moment for Seaman as he prepares to warn the Gunners about underestimating their upcoming FA Cup semi-final opponents, Reading.

Joel Burger and Ashley King, known as Burger-King since fifth grade, accepted Burger King's proposal to pay for their wedding, which includes personalized gift bags, Mason Jars, and Burger King crowns. The popular fast food chain called the engaged couple on Skype and offered to cover the costs, describing the celebration as a "whopper of a wedding" that was uniquely tailored to their love story. Their engagement photo was taken at a local Burger King, and the couple is set to wed on July 17th in Jacksonville.

A man, reportedly about 35 years old and 6 feet 3 inches tall, was found in Gniezno, Poland, suffering from amnesia and unable to recall his identity or origin. The police are asking for help in identifying him, releasing photos and information to find someone who recognizes him. His accent remains unidentified, and efforts are underway to determine his background.

Raheem Sterling has expressed his desire to leave Liverpool despite receiving a lucrative offer to extend his contract. Although he was initially interested in signing a new deal worth £100,000 per week, Sterling claims he would have accepted a lower salary a year ago. Despite being one of Liverpool's key players, the 20-year-old striker has struggled this season, scoring six goals and failing to match his impressive performances from the previous campaign.

A massive fire broke out at a former strip club building in Flint, Michigan, starting around 1 PM on a Wednesday. The incident has been investigated, but the cause is still under review. It's noted that this was not the first time the building had experienced such a fire. Photos from the scene show thick smoke and intense flames. No immediate information was available on the condition or potential damage to the building itself.

Ashley Young, celebrating his role in Manchester United's 4-1 win over Manchester City in the derby, described the raucous Manchester City supporters as "noisy neighbours." Despite initially trailing 1-0, United stormed back with goals from Juan Mata and Marouane Fellaini to secure a convincing victory, silencing the home crowd. Young emphasized that this win, United's sixth in a row, boosts the team's confidence and belief that they can win any match and finish the season strongly.

Backache among younger individuals is on the rise, affecting nearly half of those under 30 due to sedentary lifestyles and increased use of mobile devices, according to experts. The British Chiropractic Association found that 45% of under-30s surveyed reported chronic back or neck pain, up from 28% the previous year. Chiropractors attribute this trend to users leaning over smartphones and tablets, placing additional stress on the neck and spine. Additionally, modern working habits like hot-desking contribute to poor posture and strain on the back. The study indicates that Britons spend about ten hours a day sitting, exacerbating these issues.

David Villa, now playing for New York City FC, showed his support for Atletico Madrid by cheering them on during their crucial Champions League match against Real Madrid. Despite Villa briefly playing for Atletico Madrid earlier in the season, he was absent from the squad due to competition for a starting position. During the game, Villa's former club drew 0-0 with Real Madrid, while Atletico's goalkeeper Jan Oblak made an inspired performance. Villa has been enjoying his time in New York, recently becoming captain of the team, and is looking forward to having former England international Frank Lampard join him soon.

Casey Levi challenged his son Sam to eat a California roll in exchange for a $10 prize, but Sam backed down after a minute's hesitation and ran off to vomit. Sam's sister, Charlie, took over the challenge and successfully ate the roll. The video has received over 100,000 views, with many viewers finding the children's reactions cute and amusing, while some appreciated Sam's aversion to sushi.

A six-year-old girl named Nurhayada Sofia tragically fell to her death after being pulled by an escalator at a shopping center in Malaysia. The CCTV footage showed her being dragged over the side and falling five floors, sustaining horrific head injuries. Her mother, who was on the phone with her husband at the time, was reportedly inconsolable upon learning of her daughter's death. The incident has led to an investigation into potential safety violations at the mall.

Fabian Orellana, a forward for Celta Vigo, received a one-match suspension and a straight red card after he threw a lump of grass at Barcelona player Sergio Busquets during a match where Busquets was deliberately wasting time. The incident occurred during Barcelona's 1-0 victory, and Orellana's anger stemmed from Busquets's time-wasting tactics. As a result, Orellana missed his team's next match against Granada.

Fiorentina secured a 2-0 win over Sampdoria with goals from Diamanti and Salah, lifting them to fourth place with 49 points. Meanwhile, Mohamed Salah continued his impressive form for Fiorentina, scoring twice in the match. In other matches, Roma won 1-0 against Napoli, Carlos Tevez scored for Juventus against Empoli, and AC Milan won 2-1 away from home against Palermo. Juventus maintained their top spot with a 2-0 victory over Empoli.

A Vodafone customer named Adele Berry received flirtatious texts from an employee of the company's South African call center after contacting the company about a billing issue. Berry, 21, was shocked and distressed by these messages, which included requests for personal information and repeated offers of gifts. Vodafone immediately suspended the offending employee and is investigating the incident.

During the British Army's eight-year conflict in Afghanistan, they expended an enormous amount of ammunition—approximately 46 million rounds—primarily using 5.56mm rounds from SA-80 rifles and 9mm rounds from handguns. Additionally, artillery and gunship rounds were used, costing around £200 million. The high volume of ammunition use indicates intense combat conditions with close-quarters fighting and the need for constant protection and support.

In a tragic accident, a 50-year-old man named Mr. Zhang died eight months after his brother was killed in the same location by a lorry. Mr. Zhang was hit by a silver car while riding his three-wheeled motorbike through a busy junction in Dezhou City. Despite his brother's death eight months prior, Mr. Zhang survived briefly before succumbing to injuries at the hospital. The accident highlights the lack of road signs and the illegal use of the road, leading to ongoing investigations into the driver.

Firefighter Cody Knecht used a duck quacking ringtone to lure six ducklings trapped in a storm drain in Louisiana. After 90 minutes of initial efforts, he successfully rescued all six ducklings, which had previously eluded capture due to their agitation. This incident, along with a previous chimney rescue, highlights the firefighter's innovative problem-solving skills.

The head teacher of a school in India, Lalit Verma, has been arrested for allegedly beating an 11-year-old student, Shiva, to death after discovering he had stolen a pencil and eraser from another student. Shiva reportedly complained of a severe stomachache and vomited blood before dying in the hospital. This incident follows other cases where young Indian students have been beaten to death by teachers. Verma admitted to slapping Shiva during questioning, and the headteacher has been charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder. Other similar cases involving extreme corporal punishment on young students have also been reported.

Charlie Austin made a significant impression on his first day at Queens Park Rangers (QPR), where he immediately befriended Joey Barton, despite initially ignoring him. Their relationship evolved positively, and Austin scored 15 goals in the Premier League, earning him potential recognition as an England squad member. Despite QPR's current struggles, Austin emphasizes the importance of performing well in upcoming matches, including against Aston Villa, to secure points crucial for avoiding relegation.

The adoptive mother of a nine-year-old boy in Nanjing used skip ropes, metal rods, and water pipes to punish the child for not knowing the story of Pinocchio from an extra-curricular assignment, resulting in severe physical injuries and scars. This abuse was uncovered six months later after the boy's school teachers saw the worsening injuries and noticed changes in his behavior. The woman was arrested, and the child has since been returned to his biological family while receiving psychological support.

In 1964, a secret police intelligence report identified an unnamed hit band as having attended the same paedophile brothel where Jimmy Savile was known to frequent. While it's unclear if both the band and Savile visited the brothel simultaneously, the document linked them to a notorious flat in Battersea used by paedophiles. This information surfaced as part of ongoing investigations into Savile's alleged abuse activities.

During Michelle Obama's annual "Take Our Daughters and Sons to Work Day" event at the White House, a 5-year-old girl named Anya Brodie impressed the First Lady with her kind words about her youthful appearance. When Obama was asked her age, Anya said she looked too young for her, which surprised everyone present. This compliment so moved Obama that she invited Anya on stage for a hug. Many other children were also in attendance, including those from various organizations.

Midfielder Georginio Wijnaldum, currently with PSV Eindhoven, expressed interest in joining a "great club" in a major European league, speculating about potential moves to Manchester United, Arsenal, and Newcastle. While he is content at PSV and expresses ambition to play in a stronger league, he acknowledges the appeal of Premier League clubs like Manchester United, Arsenal, and Newcastle.

The British Army's 77 Brigade, established to combat Islamic State's dominance on the internet, is facing recruitment difficulties. Originally planned to be composed of 2,000 experts, the brigade will only have 454 regular and reservist troops when fully operational. The Army struggles to find individuals with specialized knowledge in social media, financial operations, and counter-criminality, which are crucial for the unit's missions. This lack of personnel has led to morale issues within the brigade, with some reservists losing their positions and uncertainty among others about their roles.

A farmer in Anhui, China, discovered that one of his goats had given birth to a baby goat with six legs. This rare "spider goat" was born with four forelegs and two hind legs, making it 20 days old. The goat, named by internet users as a "spider goat," is reportedly growing well and has a voracious appetite. It weighs 5 kg and is already quite playful, though its extra legs have become shorter over time. Despite initial concerns, the goat's unusual condition does not affect its health or growth.

In the first incident, a garbage truck driver was killed after his vehicle rolled forward, pinning him between the truck and a pole as he collected bins early on in Melbourne's west. In the second incident, three people died and one survived seriously injured after a car crash in Melbourne's east, with the car carrying six passengers. Police and hospitals are investigating both incidents.

Scott Stephenson, a 19-year-old, was due to be sentenced for stealing a phone from a man who had died of hypothermia in the cold streets of South Shields. During his court appearance, Stephenson's anger and profanity erupted when he was released on bail. He made a rude gesture at a nearby restaurant, while witnesses watched in horror. Stephenson and his friend, Dale Walker, had taken advantage of the unconscious man's condition by stealing his iPhone. Stephenson pleaded guilty to theft, while Walker admitted to handling the stolen property. Stephenson was ultimately sentenced, though it was delayed pending a pre-sentence report.

Harry Redknapp, now unemployed, was attacked with coins and verbally abused by Arsenal fans upon leaving the Emirates Stadium after an Arsenal 4-1 win against Liverpool. This incident occurred while Redknapp was a manager at his old club Tottenham, which has made him unpopular among Arsenal supporters. He described the experience as "unreal" and stated that abusive fans ruin the atmosphere for many.

Per Mertesacker, a former Arsenal defender, claims that bringing back the "arguing culture" within the team is key to their improved performance. After a series of heavy defeats last season, including a 6-3 loss to Manchester City and a 5- bleed to Liverpool, Mertesacker believes that facing each other more openly and discussing mistakes is necessary for team success. This confrontational approach has reportedly helped Arsenal turn around their fortunes this season and improve their standing in the league.

Miranda Ashley Calvin, a 19-year-old Oregon resident, was arrested for allegedly hitting and killing 49-year-old pedestrian Jerrie Ann Hornig with her car while fleeing the scene. The incident occurred on a Gresham road around 8:30 PM on Saturday. After an investigation, Calvin was arrested at a Marriott Hotel in Clackamas. Hornig's son, Maxwell Horning, shared a touching post about his mother, emphasizing the shock and sadness felt by the family. Hornig's death remains under investigation, and her son's social media tribute has brought attention to the case.

Martin Skrtel, who has been with Liverpool since 2008, has rejected speculation about moves to Wolfsburg and Napoli, stating that he won't leave Liverpool and will sign a new contract. Despite the club's recent struggles, the defender reaffirmed his commitment to staying with the Premier League team. Liverpool is reportedly preparing to make changes in the transfer market, but Skrtel insists he remains at Anfield.

A 20-year-old woman named Britney E. Montenegro was involved in a fight involving multiple individuals that began inside a bar and spilled onto the streets in Orlando, Florida. The arrest report states that Montenegro was restrained by police due to her involvement in the altercation, which reportedly included her attempting to attack another woman. Despite her injuries, including cuts and blood from the fight, she was relatively unharmed and did not suffer any significant internal injuries. Montenegro was later charged with disorderly conduct.

Lucas Moura, a Paris Saint-Germain player, expressed admiration for Lionel Messi, comparing him as his idol and stating that Messi is "the best player in the world." Moura praised Messi's playing style and admitted that PSG faces a challenging task in preventing him from scoring. The match against Barcelona is scheduled for Wednesday night, with PSG also meeting them again on April 21 at the Nou Camp.

A 49-year-old man named Eligah Christian, wanted on multiple felony charges, attempted to flee law enforcement using a 2014 motorhome in a high-speed chase in Alaska. Despite the state troopers deploying spike strips and sirens, Christian rammed several patrol cars and managed to evade capture for some time. Eventually, he was stopped and arrested at a residence off Pittman Road. Christian was charged with felony reckless driving and criminal mischief.

The 13 glowing blue letters that illuminated Yankee Stadium were not sold at an auction at Sotheby's, with a Sotheby's employee bidding $260,000 on their behalf. This sign, 10 feet tall, had been on display since 1976 and was expected to sell for between $300,000 and $600,000. It currently belongs to former Yankee Reggie Jackson, who purchased it for an undisclosed amount when the stadium ceased operations in 2008. The letters were among other items from the Mets included in the auction, which saw them sell for $112,500 instead of the estimated $20,000 to $30,00

Bob Shannon, the former trainer of Ricky Hatton, has been charged with sexually assaulting a young girl under the age of 16. Shannon trained Hatton for one fight, a loss against Vyacheslav Senchenko, after helping Matthew Hatton to a world title shot. Prior to this, Shannon trained other notable British boxers like Denton Vassell and Matthew Hatton. The charges came after a reported assault on a girl in 2014/2015, and he faces further legal proceedings.

Tottenham Hotspur is in negotiations to extend Nabil Bentaleb's contract, aiming to secure his services before his current deal expires in the summer of 2018. The club wants to reward Bentaleb for his impressive performances under coach Mauricio Pochettino. There's also concern that Bentaleb's star might draw unwanted attention from other European clubs, prompting Tottenham to move quickly on a new contract. Bentaleb's wage package is expected to significantly increase, potentially reaching around £35,000 per week.

Kenny Dalglish argues that Harry Kane, who recently earned his first England senior caps, should avoid playing for the Under-21 European Championship in order to stay fresh for Roy Hodgson's senior team in 2016. Dalglish believes Kane has already demonstrated he can handle international competition and suggests keeping him out of the Young Lions' tournament to prevent unnecessary fatigue. He also emphasizes that England's Under-21 side is meant to develop future talent, not compete for titles.

Kaka and Cyle Larin scored crucial goals as Orlando City secured a 2-0 victory over Portland Timbers at Providence Park. Larin made an early impact with a diving header, and Kaka converted a penalty kick to seal the result. The Timbers' Darlington Nagbe posed the main threat but was unable to salvage the game, helping Orlando City maintain a strong third-place position in the league.

A baggage handler at LaGuardia Airport in New York was caught on camera doing push-ups on the runway after loading a plane.
